title: Oxygen Atom Sticking to a Molecule
body: An oxygen atom (mass $$16$$ u) moving at $$733$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ at $$15.0$$ degrees with respect to the $$\hat{i}$$ direction collides and sticks to an oxygen molecule (mass $$32$$ u) moving at $$528$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ at $$128$$ degrees with respect to the $$\hat{i}$$ direction. The two stick together to form ozone.
